title: Speaking: Confessions and Accusations
learningGoal: Verbally deploy compound verbs to express completion and suddenness, while correctly dropping the compound in negative replies.
instructions: Roleplay an argument with your sibling. Accuse them of doing things using compound verbs (ate up, broke). When defending yourself, use negative sentences (remember the negative rule!).
scenario: An argument over a broken laptop and a missing pizza.

subTasks.prompt: Sibling 2: 'Who ate the pizza?' Accuse them: 'You ate up the pizza!' (You = Tumne, Ate up = Kha liya)

Sibling 2: 'No I didn't! Did you break my laptop?' Defend yourself using a negative: 'I did not break your laptop.' (I = Maine, Your laptop = Tumhara laptop, Did not break = Nahi toda - DO NOT USE DAALA)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: I did not break your laptop.

subTasks.prompt: Sibling 2: 'Then how is it broken?' Tell them what happened: 'It suddenly fell down.' (It = Yeh, Suddenly = Achanak, Fell down = Gir pada)
Sibling 2 starts crying. Tell them: 'Don't cry. I will bring a new one.' (Don't cry = Mat ro, I = Main, New = Naya, Will bring = Le aaunga)

sampleResponse.targetText: मत रो। मैं नया ले आऊँगा।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Mat ro. Main naya le aaunga.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Don't cry. I will bring a new one.

scenarioContext.targetText: आप अपने भाई/बहन से बहस कर रहे हैं।
scenarioContext.transliteration: Aap apne bhai/behan se bahas kar rahe hain.
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: You are arguing with your brother/sister.

baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: I did not break your laptop.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: मत रो। मैं नया ले आऊँगा।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mat ro. Main naya le aaunga.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Don't cry. I will bring a new one.

acceptableParaphrases: Saying 'Gir gaya' instead of 'Gir pada' is acceptable, though 'pada' is better for suddenness.

criteria.name: Negative Compound Error
criteria.description: If user says 'Maine nahi tod daala', deduct heavily. They must drop the 'daala' because it's a negative sentence.

criteria.name: Vector Matching
criteria.description: If user says 'Kha diya', deduct points. Eating is for self-benefit, so it must be 'Kha liya'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Tumne pizza kha liya! Maine tumhara laptop nahi toda. Yeh achanak gir pada. Mat ro. Main naya le aaunga.

feedbackRules.onPass: Incredible! You handled the nuances of inward action (liya), sudden action (pada), and the negative rule flawlessly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Section 6 of the lesson. You cannot use 'Daala' or 'Liya' when saying 'Nahi'. It must be 'Nahi toda'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Watch out for 'Kha liya'. Because you eat food into your own body, the vector verb must be 'Lena' (took), never 'Dena' (gave).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: तुमने पिज़्ज़ा खा लिया! मैंने तुम्हारा लैपटॉप नहीं तोड़ा। यह अचानक गिर पड़ा। मत रो। मैं नया ले आऊँगा।

hintText: Speak Sentence 2 with confidence. Just use the simple verb: 'Nahi toda'. Do not complicate it.
